element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Player can intercept enemy bullets with fists
Spent at platforms to purchase health, attack power, or extra lives
Three platform types: health (300 coins), attack power (100 coins), extra life (2000 coins)

element_introduction_order.element: Letter icons (α and β robotic parts)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Collect three letters to spawn robotic satellite companion; eight possible combinations with different attack patterns
Satellites attack alongside player, can be used for hovering; each combination grants unique abilities (e.g., ααβ fires lasers, αβα uses sword)
Collecting same letter combination twice in succession while holding a robot triggers temporary enhanced form with more powerful attacks
Final stage becomes accessible only after mastering all six accessible stages in any order

typical_relief_triggers: Activating transformation fusion with enhanced attacks
typical_tension_triggers: Robot companion sustains damage and risks destruction
Low health without coin accumulation for restoration
Enemy bullet patterns requiring punch interception
Stage progression to any of six mid-game areas
Approaching final locked stage Area G
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Non-linear middle structure (Areas B-F selectable in any order) provides player agency and relief from forced escalation, contrasted by gated final stage that creates late-game tension spike

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Coin platforms scattered throughout stages; robotic part letter icons appear in destructible containers
spatial_grammar.secret_convention: Letter icon combinations yield eight distinct satellite variants; same-combo-twice triggers fusion transformation

difficulty_curve.early_game: Area A serves as introductory factory stage teaching all core mechanics: punching, coin collection, platform purchasing, letter icon system, and satellite companions
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Areas B-F present selectable non-linear progression allowing player choice; regional variant (Solbrain) shows Area C carnival is 'much easier' while Shatterhand submarine version presents higher challenge; regional enemy HP differences suggest difficulty calibration across versions
difficulty_curve.late_game: Area G becomes mandatory final stage after completing all six accessible areas; Japanese Solbrain features futuristic makeover of final level in contrast to industrial US Shatterhand version; suggests visual but likely mechanical difficulty increase
notable_spikes: Area A to any of Areas B-F: introduction to stage-specific enemy patterns and layouts
Area C submarine (Shatterhand) vs carnival (Solbrain): substantial difficulty variance between regional versions
Area G unlock: progression gate requiring completion of six prior stages before accessing final confrontation

notable_level_patterns.description: Area A functions as isolated introductory stage teaching all mechanics (punch, coins, platforms, letters, satellites) before releasing player into six freely-selectable mid-game areas
Areas B through F can be completed in any order, granting player agency and reducing forced pacing; this contrasts with gated final area design

Solbrain (JP) features longer transformation duration (68 seconds vs standard), higher enemy HP variance, easier Area C carnival vs harder submarine, and modified boss AI; indicates developer deliberate difficulty balancing across releases
Area G becomes accessible only after mastering all six prerequisite stages, creating final narrative and mechanical escalation point
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Area G (Final Stage, unlocks after A-F)

sources_used: tcrf (The Cutting Room Floor - Shatterhand article)
